generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LiDAR sensor mounted on the vehicle, generating point cloud XYZ with timestamps and reflectance values."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudTransmitted
]

instance CameraSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Onboard camera capturing high‑resolution images for visual perception."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CameraImageTransmitted
]

instance VehicleStateBusSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle state bus sensor reading vehicle kinematic parameters: speed, acceleration, steering angle."
    DTDFVocab:producedData KinematicStateTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance CameraImageTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CameraSensor
]

instance KinematicStateTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateBusSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point cloud data containing XYZ coordinates with timestamps and reflectance."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LiDARProcessingEnabler
]

instance CameraImageData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw camera image data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CameraImageTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance KinematicStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle kinematic state data including speed, acceleration, steering angle."
    DTDFVocab:fromData KinematicStateTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VehicleStateProcessingEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrackData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Extracted object track data from LiDAR point cloud clustering."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ComputationOffloadEnabler
]

instance DesiredSpeedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Computed desired speed from ACC algorithm executed on cloud."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudTransmitted
]

// C11: Enablers
instance LiDARProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processes raw LiDAR point cloud to extract 2D plane representation and object tracking."
    DTDFVocab:enables ObjectTrackingService
]

instance VehicleStateProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Updates vehicle state model based on kinematic data."
    DTDFVocab:enables VehicleStateModelService
]

instance ComputationOffloadEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enables offloading of ACC computation to cloud."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance RemoteManagementEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Facilitates remote enabling/disabling of ACC and emergency braking."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Serializes sensor data into ROS2‑compatible messages and aligns units."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

// C6: Services
instance ObjectTrackingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that provides extracted object tracks."
    DTDFVocab:provides ObjectTrackData
]

instance VehicleStateModelService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that models vehicle state."
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that executes ACC algorithm on cloud and returns desired speed."
    DTDFVocab:provides DesiredSpeedData
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCPerformanceInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that handles remote commands for ACC control and emergency braking."
    DTDFVocab:provides StartACCAction
    DTDFVocab:provides StopACCAction
    DTDFVocab:provides EmergencyBrakeAction
]

instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "User interface service displaying real‑time vehicle state, projected path, object tracks and allows reporting."
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCPerformanceInsight
]

// C17: Insights / Actions
instance ACCPerformanceInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight showing ACC performance metrics such as desired speed vs. actual speed."
]

instance VehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight of current vehicle state."
]

instance StartACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to enable ACC on the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance StopACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to disable ACC on the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action to trigger emergency braking."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control decisions made within the Digital Twin are sent downstream to the Physical Twin using the same Wi‑Fi UDP channel. Commands comprise serialized motor actuation commands and steering servo actuation commands that specify target torque or angle values. These commands are published on ROS2 topics that map to the vehicle’s actuation interfaces. The document does not provide information on authentication, authorization, or cryptographic validation of these messages. Feedback from the vehicle’s state buses (e.g., current speed, steering angle) is fed back into the digital model to confirm command execution, but explicit mechanisms for conflict resolution or command acknowledgment are not described."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The digital twin’s temporal coordination is driven by a 100 ms clock synchronization accuracy requirement. All computing nodes and physical devices must share a common notion of time so that data and control signals are exchanged within a 100 ms envelope. The system does not specify higher‑frequency sync loops, but it acknowledges that stricter (<100 ms) synchronization is mandated by safety‑critical vehicular standards for which the platform would need to support if required. Latency constraints for individual messaging are not detailed in the document."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The overall orchestration follows the ISO 23247 Reference Architecture, mapping each software feature entity (FE) to a ROS 2 package that contains one or more ROS 2 nodes. The system is organized into three hierarchical entities: User Entity (contains a User Interface FE split into a 3D Visualization and Control Panel), Digital Twin Entity (hosts data management and control mechanisms), and Physical Twin Entity (hosts device communication, data pre‑processing, actuation, and general automotive functions such as point‑cloud clustering & tracking). Each entity is mapped to a distinct physical node, providing decentralized deployment while maintaining a coherent whole through ROS 2 message passing. Resource management is achieved by assigning specific FEs to dedicated hardware platforms; for example, the Physical Twin Entity sits on the vehicle’s compute unit, whereas the User Entity runs on a separate workstation. System‑wide coordination is performed via ROS 2 topics, services, and actions that implement the data flow between sensors, actuators, and digital‑twin logic. The architecture departs from the ISO 23247 standard in that the Physical Twin Entity reflects the inherent resources (compute, sensors, actuators) rather than being an abstract entity; this enables hosting of automotive‑specific functionality."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The digital twin infrastructure employs a dual‑layer communication architecture. Inter‑SW component communication occurs over a ROS2 network, which aggregates messages using the DDS protocol on a local Ethernet or in‑vehicle network. For inter‑entity communication between the Physical Twin Entity and the Digital Twin Entity, a Wi‑Fi network transmits data using UDP. The document does not specify bandwidth, latency, reliability, redundancy, or security protocols, but the choice of UDP implies a low‑latency, best‑effort transfer suitable for real‑time sensor feeds. The ROS2 layer relies on DDS Quality of Service settings (e.g., reliability, durability) to meet internal communication requirements."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The system complies with ISO 23247 – Digital twin framework for manufacturing. It adopts the reference architecture defined in Part 2, which specifies an entity‑based hierarchy of Data Collection & Device Control, Digital Twin and User entities, each composed of functional entities. The implementation may leverage OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA) for data collection, device control, and information exchange, thereby ensuring interoperability with standard industrial communication protocols."
]
